_description: Realistic parameters derived from BUPT-1 (Tiansuan Constellation) hardware measurements.
_note: Fields prefixed with '_' are metadata only — the solver ignores them. Rollback by pointing to config_base_old.json.

_units.alpha: dimensionless — cost weight for processor nodes
_units.beta: dimensionless — cost weight for communication nodes
_units.R_max: bytes/s — max ISL/downlink link rate (X-band, 20 Mbps)
_units.T_comm: seconds — communication window duration per pass
_units.T_comp: seconds — total computation window (one orbit segment)
_units.N: dimensionless — max total nodes (upper bound for solver)
_units.E_processor: Wh (watt-hours) — energy budget per processor node for the full T_comp window
_units.E_comm: Wh (watt-hours) — energy budget per communication node for the full T_comm window
_units.p: Wh/FLOP — energy cost per floating-point op (FP16, Tensor Cores)
_units.q: Wh/byte — energy cost per transmitted byte (X-band link)

_derivations.R_max: X-band target rate: 20 Mbps = 20e6 bits/s / 8 = 2,500,000 bytes/s. Expressed in bytes/s to match D (bytes) in layer JSON files, keeping the bandwidth constraint I*D <= (x+y)*R_max*T_comm dimensionally consistent.
_derivations.E_processor: 10% of per-orbit usable battery energy allocated to processing satellite: 0.10 * 0.25 * 115 Wh = 2.875 Wh, rounded to 2.9 Wh. Represents the fraction of battery depth-of-discharge (25% DoD target on 115 Wh battery) that one processing satellite can spend on inference per orbit.
_derivations.E_comm: 10% of per-orbit usable battery energy allocated to communication satellite: 0.10 * 0.25 * 115 Wh = 2.875 Wh, rounded to 2.9 Wh. Same DoD-based budget as E_processor but for inter-satellite link / downlink transmission.
_derivations.p: FP16 Tensor Cores: 15 W / 10 TFLOPS = 1.5e-12 J/FLOP. Converted to Wh: 1.5e-12 / 3600 = 4.17e-16 Wh/FLOP.
_derivations.q: X-band: 38 W at 100 Mbps = 12.5 MB/s => 38 / 12.5e6 = 3.04e-6 J/byte. Converted to Wh: 3.04e-6 / 3600 = 8.44e-10 Wh/byte.
